summary: Derived valuation metrics from Polymarket 'closing market cap above $X' YES-midpoint prices. Probabilities are market midpoints; metrics are interpolated from the implied survival function P(cap > X).

implied_median.method: Linear interpolation of the threshold where the implied CDF P(cap > X) crosses 0.50.
assumptions: Monotonic survival curve between bracketing thresholds.
implied_median.sensitivity_note: Robust: depends only on the two thresholds straddling 50%, not on tail shape.

implied_mean.method: Bucket-weighted expected value: sum of (bucket probability x bucket midpoint) across all buckets.
assumptions: Within-bucket mass sits at the bucket midpoint.
implied_mean.sensitivity_note: Assumption-sensitive: the two tail-midpoint offsets materially affect the result when tail probabilities are non-trivial. Treat as approximate.

iqr.method: p25/p75 valuations = interpolated thresholds where P(cap > X) crosses 0.75 and 0.25 respectively (the 50% confidence band).
assumptions: Monotonic survival curve between bracketing thresholds.
iqr.sensitivity_note: Robust: interpolation between observed thresholds; null when the band extends beyond the quoted range.

bucket_prob.method: P(cap > this threshold) - P(cap > next threshold); top bucket = P(cap > top). The '< lowest' bucket = 1 - P(cap > lowest).
assumptions: All buckets including '< lowest' sum to 1.0 (enforced by validation).
bucket_prob.sensitivity_note: Direct differences of observed probabilities; no modelling assumptions.

confidence.method: Worst-of three signals: active-threshold count, monotonicity-violation count, and mean best_ask-best_bid spread. Tier capped at 'medium' when order-book (spread) data is unavailable (price-only history).
assumptions: high: >=12 thresholds AND 0 violations AND mean spread < 0.04.
medium: 8-11 thresholds, OR <=2 violations, OR spread 0.04-0.08, OR price-only.
low: <8 thresholds, OR >2 violations, OR spread > 0.08.
confidence.sensitivity_note: Backfilled history is price-only (no book) and therefore capped at 'medium'.

raw_sha256.method: sha256 over the canonical JSON serialization of raw_inputs (fixed key order: token_id, threshold, midpoint, best_bid, best_ask, volume; ascending by threshold; literal API string values).
assumptions: Consumers re-serialize the stored raw_inputs identically to reproduce the hash.
raw_sha256.sensitivity_note: Provenance check, not a metric: proves derived numbers came from the stored, unaltered inputs.

changelog.change: Initial published methodology: median, mean (with tail assumptions), IQR band, bucket probabilities, three-signal confidence, and raw_sha256 provenance.
